title: Vilfa rupestris

The exact classification of Vilfa rupestris within the Poaceae family is within the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Within the tribe Poeae, the genus Vilfa is a part of the subtribe Vilfinae. intro: Vilfa rupestris, also known as Mountain Vilfa, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, thanks to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa rupestris include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vilfa rupestris include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory infections.
